findings.title: Positive rebases desynchronize the AMM pair balance and let sellers extract excess ETH

best_agent_match.title: Positive rebases desynchronize the AMM pair balance and enable ETH drain

findings.title: Pre-live buys can permanently blacklist arbitrary victim addresses

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can permanently blacklist arbitrary victims before launch by buying to them

findings.title: Dust buys can repeatedly reset a holder's cooldown and block timely sells

best_agent_match.title: Dust buys can repeatedly freeze any holder for 5 minutes

findings.title: Anyone can permanently disable the intended pre-launch protection

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can permanently blacklist arbitrary victims before launch by buying to them

findings.title: The max-wallet check uses the pre-buy balance, so buyers can exceed the cap

best_agent_match.title: The max-wallet check is performed before the incoming buy, so wallets can exceed the cap

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can blacklist any address to permanently block transfers
rejected_candidates.reason: The owner has no function that sets `blacklist[address] = true`; blacklist entries are only created automatically on pair-to-user buys while `_live` is false.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited token supply expansion via rebase function
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract does have a positive rebase, but the standalone 'unlimited minting' claim is overstated and does not by itself show protocol-level loss; the real exploitable consequence is the LP balance desynchronization captured in F-001.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner bypasses all transaction limits
rejected_candidates.reason: The owner exemption is an explicit privileged design choice, not a distinct vulnerability absent a stronger trust-minimization requirement.

rejected_candidates.title: No timelock on critical ownership functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Lack of a timelock is a governance/design preference and is not a concrete exploitable bug in this contract.

rejected_candidates.title: First-time buyers bypass time restriction
rejected_candidates.reason: `_buyInfo == 0` only exempts addresses that have never bought from the pair; that matches the implemented cooldown model rather than exposing a permissionless bypass against tracked buyers.

rejected_candidates.title: Deprecated Solidity version 0.6.0
rejected_candidates.reason: Using an older compiler version is too generic to be a reportable finding without a contract-specific exploit path.

rejected_candidates.title: Permanent ownership renouncement possible
rejected_candidates.reason: `renounceOwnership()` is standard Ownable behavior; the claimed harm is speculative and not a vulnerability by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Inconsistent taxFee parameter not used
rejected_candidates.reason: `taxFee` is always passed as zero, so this is dead tokenomics code rather than a security-impacting issue.
